content:
## MARKET OVERVIEW

**1. What is the Current Market Regime?**
RISK-ON: low volatility 19.7% and full breadth with 8/8 sectors positive, broad-based risk appetite and rotation into cyclicals.

**2. Which sectors are leading?**
Energy & Chemicals leading with big winners HEKTS (1W: +29.45% | 1M: +54.17%) ABOVE TREND and SASA (1W: +12.10% | 1M: +44.86%) ABOVE TREND. Heavy Industry is strong with EREGL (1W: +17.48% | 1M: +43.07%) ABOVE TREND and TOASO (1W: +8.74% | 1M: +27.08%) ABOVE TREND; Retail & Food showing strength via CCOLA (1W: +15.53% | 1M: +29.33%) ABOVE TREND.

**3. Which sectors are lagging?**
Transportation is the relative lag with TAVHL (1W: +1.97% | 1M: -9.19%) RECOVERY and THYAO (1W: +0.89% | 1M: +6.51%) ABOVE TREND but shallow. Banking trails the rally versus cyclicals despite gains: ISCTR (1W: +1.46% | 1M: +11.07%) ABOVE TREND and VAKBN (1W: +1.07% | 1M: +3.49%) ABOVE TREND.

## STOCK PERFORMANCE

**4. Top Performing Stocks?**
HEKTS (1W: +29.45% | 1M: +54.17%) ABOVE TREND; EREGL (1W: +17.48% | 1M: +43.07%) ABOVE TREND; CCOLA (1W: +15.53% | 1M: +29.33%) ABOVE TREND; GUBRF (1W: +14.42% | 1M: +28.06%) ABOVE TREND; SASA (1W: +12.10% | 1M: +44.86%) ABOVE TREND.

**5. Worst Performing Stocks?**
TUPRS (1W: -4.61% | 1M: -0.58%) BELOW TREND; FROTO (1W: -0.51% | 1M: +0.83%) CORRECTION; KONTR (1W: -0.19% | 1M: +33.55%) CORRECTION; OYAKC (1W: -0.17% | 1M: +0.60%) CORRECTION; THYAO (1W: +0.89% | 1M: +6.51%) ABOVE TREND.

**6. Momentum Stocks?**
HEKTS (1W: +29.45% | 1M: +54.17%) ABOVE TREND; EREGL (1W: +17.48% | 1M: +43.07%) ABOVE TREND; CCOLA (1W: +15.53% | 1M: +29.33%) ABOVE TREND; GUBRF (1W: +14.42% | 1M: +28.06%) ABOVE TREND; TKFEN (1W: +8.20% | 1M: +62.25%) ABOVE TREND.

## SECTOR OUTLOOK

**7. Banking Sector Outlook?**
Banking participation is steady but muted relative to cyclicals; AKBNK (1W: +2.80% | 1M: +8.66%) ABOVE TREND, GARAN (1W: +2.62% | 1M: +8.03%) ABOVE TREND and YKBNK (1W: +6.10% | 1M: +14.44%) ABOVE TREND — prefer selective exposure to higher-beta lenders.

**8. Industrial Sector Outlook?**
Heavy industry momentum intact: EREGL (1W: +17.48% | 1M: +43.07%) ABOVE TREND, TOASO (1W: +8.74% | 1M: +27.08%) ABOVE TREND and ASELS (1W: +1.96% | 1M: +25.29%) ABOVE TREND; FROTO (1W: -0.51% | 1M: +0.83%) CORRECTION is the notable diverger.

**9. Energy & Chemicals Outlook?**
Sector leadership clear but bifurcated: SASA (1W: +12.10% | 1M: +44.86%) ABOVE TREND and HEKTS (1W: +29.45% | 1M: +54.17%) ABOVE TREND are momentum names while TUPRS (1W: -4.61% | 1M: -0.58%) BELOW TREND is a weak spot to avoid or short selectively.

## PORTFOLIO STRATEGY

**10. Portfolio Recommendation?**
Risk-on stance: overweight Energy & Chemicals, Heavy Industry and high-beta Retail, trim exposure to lagging Transportation and underperforming TUPRS; keep stop discipline on momentum names and lock partial profits into strength.

## NEXT WEEK OUTLOOK

**11. Stocks to Watch Next Week?**
TKFEN (1W: +8.20% | 1M: +62.25%) ABOVE TREND for continuation or profit-taking; HEKTS (1W: +29.45% | 1M: +54.17%) ABOVE TREND for momentum confirmation; EREGL (1W: +17.48% | 1M: +43.07%) ABOVE TREND as a sector bellwether; TUPRS (1W: -4.61% | 1M: -0.58%) BELOW TREND for downside risk and mean-reversion signals.

**12. Sectors to Watch Next Week?**
Energy & Chemicals for follow-through and earnings/oil-driven moves; Heavy Industry for cyclical leadership and flow; Construction & REIT for domestic demand confirmation (TKFEN, ENKAI).

**13. Key Levels & Triggers?**
Watch XU100 momentum vs its 20-day moving average for continuation of risk-on flows and any failure as a near-term sell trigger; catalysts: domestic CPI/CBRT commentary, global risk appetite (US rates), oil price swings and company-specific earnings/updates.

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

Risk-on market with low volatility (19.7%) and universal sector breadth; cyclicals — Energy & Chemicals and Heavy Industry — lead the rally while Transportation and select energy names (TUPRS) lag. Trade overweight momentum names with strict stops, watch next week for earnings/oil and central bank cues that could puncture short-term strength.
